summary.intent: Build a React Native/Expo web app with an Express+WebSocket API server for the Gossamer project, displaying Claude Code sessions and events from a local SQLite database in real time, then iteratively improve the UI.
summary.outcome: Fully implemented the backend API server, Expo web frontend, session linking (bidirectional plan→implementation), scrolling, PreToolUse+PostToolUse collapsing, and importance-weighted event rendering with a custom markdown renderer.

repo: Gossamer uses Prisma 7 + better-sqlite3 for SQLite; all local imports in src/ require `.js` extensions due to NodeNext module resolution
Two separate tsconfigs must be maintained: root (NodeNext) for src/ and app/tsconfig.json (expo/tsconfig.base) — they must not be merged
state.agentParents is the existing mechanism for parent session tracking; plan→implementation linking hooks into this same map

When Claude Code approves a plan, it fires SessionEnd on the plan session and SessionStart on the new implementation session but never fires UserPromptSubmit for the approval message — the initial prompt is permanently missing from the DB
Plan→implementation session links can be detected by finding a SessionEnd in the same cwd within 30 seconds before a SessionStart
childSessionIds is derived at query time from parentSessionId — no schema change needed; GET /api/sessions builds a childMap in one in-memory pass

code.finding: Must use session?.childSessionIds?.map() not session?.childSessionIds.map() — optional chaining must guard all levels including array fields that may be absent in stale API responses

useWindowDimensions().height - useHeaderHeight() provides the exact bounded height needed for scrollable containers inside @react-navigation/stack screens on web; combined with overflow:'scroll' on a plain View
COL_WIDTHS must be exported from SessionRow and imported by the header in ActiveSessions to keep columns aligned — mixing flex:1 rows with fixed-pixel headers causes misalignment
GET /api/sessions/:id requires a parallel Promise.all query for children (db.session.findMany where parentSessionId matches) to populate childSessionIds on the single-session endpoint

workflow: @react-navigation/stack renders screens in absolute-positioned divs on web; flex:1 inside resolves to 0 height so ScrollView and FlatList never get a bounded viewport and don't scroll — fix requires explicit pixel height via useWindowDimensions + useHeaderHeight
react@18.3.2 does not exist on npm; Expo 52 requires 18.3.1
@expo/metro-runtime is required for Expo web but is not included in the base package.json — must be added via npx expo install
The API server must be restarted after a rebuild to pick up new response fields; stale servers silently return old shapes causing runtime crashes in the UI
Dynamic import of server.ts from handler.ts (serve subcommand) prevents express/ws from loading on every hook invocation — important for hook performance

friction: react@18.3.2 was specified in the plan but does not exist on npm; caused npm install failure
@expo/metro-runtime was missing from the initial app/package.json and caused Expo web to fail silently
Scrolling required three separate attempts: FlatList swap failed, nested ScrollView swap failed, root cause (absolute-positioned stack screens on web) only identified on third attempt
Port 3000 already in use during smoke-testing caused confusing EADDRINUSE errors that masked successful server startup on a different port
Stale API server (pre-rebuild) caused a crash in SessionDetail because childSessionIds was undefined; the optional chaining bug (?.map vs .map) was subtle and only caught after the server was rebuilt
open_items: MarkdownView.tsx is a hand-rolled renderer using only RN primitives; a proper library (e.g. react-native-markdown-display) would handle edge cases better
ToolUseItem falls back to raw JSON (capped at 1200 chars) for unknown tool types — per-tool rendering only covers Edit, Write, Read, Bash, Glob, Grep

The first UserPromptSubmit of any implementation session started via plan approval is permanently absent from the DB and cannot be recovered — the session's summary/prompt will remain null until a subsequent message fires
RefreshControl pull-to-refresh in ActiveSessions was mentioned in the plan but may not be functional on web (RN web doesn't support native pull-to-refresh gesture)
